patterns.name: freemium-gate
patterns.description: Offer a generous free tier to drive adoption, gate advanced features behind paid plans to convert power users.

patterns.name: usage-based-pricing
patterns.description: Charge based on actual usage (seats, API calls, storage) so customers pay proportionally to value received.

patterns.name: viral-referral-loop
patterns.description: Build sharing into the product workflow so every user action potentially exposes the product to non-users.

patterns.name: land-and-expand
patterns.description: Start with a small foothold in an account (single team or department) and expand organically as the value becomes visible.

patterns.name: switching-cost-lock-in
patterns.description: Increase switching costs through data accumulation, integrations, and workflow dependencies so churn becomes painful.

patterns.name: ecosystem-play
patterns.description: Build a marketplace or integration ecosystem that makes the product a platform, not just a tool.

patterns.name: vertical-solution
patterns.description: Tailor the product deeply for a specific industry or use case rather than competing on breadth.

patterns.name: community-led-growth
patterns.description: Invest in community (forums, events, user groups) so that user relationships and knowledge-sharing drive retention and acquisition.

patterns.name: content-seo-moat
patterns.description: Create high-value educational content that ranks on search, becoming the go-to resource and driving organic inbound leads.

patterns.name: ai-assisted-workflow
patterns.description: Embed AI into core workflows to automate repetitive tasks, reduce friction, and create a defensible experience competitors can't easily copy.

patterns.name: tiered-enterprise
patterns.description: Offer clear tiers (starter, pro, enterprise) with increasing security, support, and compliance features to capture different buyer personas.

patterns.name: open-source-core
patterns.description: Release the core product as open source to build trust and community, then monetize managed hosting, enterprise features, or support.

patterns.name: gamification-loop
patterns.description: Add progress bars, streaks, achievements, or leaderboards to increase engagement and habit formation.

patterns.name: partnership-distribution
patterns.description: Partner with complementary tools or platforms to access their user base through co-marketing, integrations, or bundled offerings.

patterns.name: retention-first-monetization
patterns.description: Prioritize engagement and retention metrics over immediate revenue; optimize for long-term LTV before turning on aggressive monetization.
